name: Bruges

overview.description: Bruges' entire historic centre is a UNESCO World Heritage Site, celebrated for its canals, cobbled streets and well-preserved medieval architecture. It's one of Belgium's top day-trip and short-stay destinations for visitors.
overview.foundedNote: Prominent trading city since the 12th-13th century
whyVisit: Canal boat tours through the 'Venice of the North'
Belfry of Bruges with panoramic city views

attractions.name: Markt Square & Belfry
attractions.description: Central square dominated by the 13th-century Belfry tower.

attractions.name: Bruges Canals
attractions.description: Boat tours offering views of historic bridges and facades.

attractions.name: Basilica of the Holy Blood
attractions.description: Romanesque and Gothic basilica housing a relic venerated since the Crusades.

transportation.description: The historic centre is compact and best explored on foot.
tips: Most sights are within a 20-minute walk of the Markt

transportation.description: Direct trains from Brussels (~1 hour) make Bruges an easy day trip.

hotels.name: Historic Centre
hotels.notes: Charming but can be crowded with day-trippers; book well ahead in summer.

foodSpots.name: Vegetarian-friendly cafés
foodSpots.notes: Fewer Indian restaurants than Brussels/Antwerp — most visitors day-trip in from larger cities.

tips: Bruges gets very crowded with day-trippers — visit early morning or stay overnight for a quieter experience.
Many streets are cobblestone; comfortable footwear is essential.
Book popular restaurants and canal tours in advance during peak season.
